PLAINTIFFS (1) RESPONSE TO DEFENDANTS SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS AND (2) SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS (TRANSACTION ID # 63188729) FILED BY PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO A MUNICIPAL CORPORATION THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, BY AND THROUGH DENNIS J. HERRERA, CITY ATTORNEY FOR THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT LEM-RAY PROPERTIES I DE, LLC, CHUCK POST MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED (SUBJECT TO RECEIPT AND REVIEW OF THE ORDER). THE COURT NOTES PLAINTIFF FAILED TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1350(B)(". . . IF SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS SOUGHT, WHETHER SEPARATELY OR AS AN ALTERNATIVE TO THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, THE SPECIFIC CAUSE OF ACTION, AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE, CLAIMS FOR DAMAGES, OR ISSUES OF DUTY MUST BE STATED SPECIFICALLY IN THE NOTICE OF MOTION AND BE REPEATED, VERBATIM, IN THE SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS"). HOWEVER, THE COURT FINDS THAT THE MOTION SHOULD BE DENIED ON ITS MERITS. THE CLEAR LANGUAGE OF THE STIPULATION DOES NOT MANDATE THAT PLAINTIFFS FILE A MOTION AS THE ONLY MEANS OF OBTAINING PENALTIES IN THE EVENT THAT A VIOLATION OF THE INJUNCTION OCCURRED. (SEE STIPULATED INJUNCTION, 18:19-25; STIPULATED PERMANENT INJUNCTION, 5:9-12.) JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: N. WONG, NOT REPORTED (D501)
CASE ASSIGNED TO JUDICIAL MEDIATION BEFORE JUDGE HAROLD E KAHN. JUDICIAL MEDIATION SET FOR JUL-23-2019 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 505. NOTICE SENT BY COURT

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT LEM-RAY PROPERTIES I DE, LLC, CHUCK POST DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR AND DEFENDANT IS GIVEN ADDITIONAL 30 DAYS TO RESPOND TO THE COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO COMPLY WITH ITS OBLIGATIONS UNDER CCP 430.41(A)(1) ("[T]HE PARTY WHO FILED THE COMPLAINT, CROSS-COMPLAINT, OR ANSWER SHALL PROVIDE LEGAL SUPPORT FOR ITS POSITION THAT THE PLEADING IS LEGALLY SUFFICIENT ") UNTIL FILING OF THE OPPOSITION TO THE DEMURRER. THE COURT, THEREFORE, CONSTRUES THE OPPOSITION TO BE PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSE TO DEFENDANTS' MEET AND CONFER EFFORTS. THE PARTIES ARE TO IMMEDIATELY ENGAGE IN FURTHER MEET AND CONFER IN LIGHT OF THE PLAINTIFF'S ULTIMATE COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 430.41(A)(1). PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (D501)
ORDER DENYING DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-25-2019 Continued To Nov-19-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES: Bradley A. Russi, Esq. appearing for Plaintiff. The CRC 3.1385 Order to Show Cause is re-set to NOV-19-2019 at 10:30 AM in Department 610. Judge: GARRETT L. WONG; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Proceedings not reported. (610)

JUDICIAL MEDIATION
Off Calendar Jul-23-2019 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar May-16-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Milly Alarcon and Bradley Russi, Esq. of City and County of San Francisco, 415-554-3894, appearing for City and County of San Francisco, Plaintiff and opposing party.

Edward Singer, Esq., 650-393-5862, appearing for Chuck Post, for Defendant and moving party.

Argued. Mr. Singer stated that he contested the tentative ruling, that an injunction is treated the same as a contract, requested that the Court take notice of the motion for injunction and argued that plaintiff's attempted to file these disputes after Judge Munter retired. Ms. Alarcon stated that back in 2011 the parties had a preference to have matters in this case heard by Judge Munter and that the language did not make it mandatory that the matters be heard only by Judge Munter.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT LEM-RAY PROPERTIES I DE, LLC, CHUCK POST MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED (Subject to receipt and review of the order). The Court notes Plaintiff failed to comply with CRC 3.1350(b)(". . . If summary adjudication is sought, whether separately or as an alternative to the motion for summary judgment, the specific cause of action, affirmative defense, claims for damages, or issues of duty must be stated specifically in the notice of motion and be repeated, verbatim, in the separate statement of undisputed material facts").

However, the Court finds that the motion should be DENIED on its merits. The clear language of the stipulation does not mandate that Plaintiffs file a motion as the only means of obtaining penalties in the event that a violation of the injunction occurred. (See Stipulated Injunction, 18:19-25; Stipulated Permanent Injunction, 5:9-12.)

Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Sara Eisenberg, Deputy City attorney and Bradley Russi, Deputy City attorney (415-554-3857/415-554-4645) for Plaintiff/Moving party CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA.
Edward Singer, Esq. (650-393-5862) for Defendant/opposing party Chuck Post.
Court reporter Nancy Presant-McDonald, CSR 906, 818-551-7300, Reported (501).

The Court announces his concern re: section 8 and behaviour. Both sides Argued. Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction is taken under submission. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court will focus on whether a problem still exists. Both sides to submit proposed orders. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Nancy Presant-McDonald, CSR 906, 818-551-7300, Reported (501).

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-22-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Attorney Edward C. Singer 650-393-5862 for Defendant Chuck M. Post individually and DBA apartmentsinsf.com the moving party.
Deputy City Attorneys Sara J. Eisenberg and Bradley A. Russi 415-554-3857 for Plaintiff City and County of San Francisco, A Municipal Corporation the opposing party.

After hearing arguments from both parties in the matter of DEFENDANT LEM-RAY PROPERTIES I DE, LLC, CHUCK POST DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT; The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Moving party failed to sustain its burned and show preemption (Browne v. County of Tehama (2013) 213 Cal.App.4th 719) either under "contradiction" or "filed of exclusivity" tests. (City of Riverside v. Inland Empire (2013) 56 Cal.4th 729; Citizens for Uniform Laws v. County of Contra Costa (1991) 233 Cal. App. 3d 1468; Rental Housing Association v. City of Oakland (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 741). Order signed and filed in open court. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk J. Rumsey, Reporter Nancy E. Presant-McDonald, CSR 906. (501)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-18-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT LEM-RAY PROPERTIES I DE, LLC, CHUCK POST DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR and Defendant is given additional 30 days to respond to the complaint. Plaintiff failed to comply with its obligations under CCP 430.41(a)(1) ("[t]he party who filed the complaint, cross-complaint, or answer shall provide legal support for its position that the pleading is legally sufficient ") until filing of the opposition to the demurrer. The Court, therefore, construes the opposition to be Plaintiff's response to Defendants' meet and confer efforts. The parties are to immediately engage in further meet and confer in light of the Plaintiff's ultimate compliance with CCP 430.41(a)(1). Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)
